directional.premise: In imaging and inverse-problem theory (Iagaru 2026), hallucinated reconstructions arise precisely when the forward operator has a non-trivial null space, so components of the solution unconstrained by the data are filled in arbitrarily.
directional.conclusion: LLM hallucination can be framed as an ill-posed inverse problem: the model inverts a forward operator with a non-trivial null space, and the unconstrained null-space components surface as fabrications.

directional.premise: Regularization of an ill-posed inverse problem works by adding external constraints (a prior) that restrict the solution to a lower-dimensional subspace, damping components the data alone cannot determine.
directional.conclusion: Retrieval-augmented generation acts as a regularizer on the retrieval inverse problem, injecting a low-rank prior that suppresses the unconstrained (hallucinated) components.

directional.premise: Git is the version-control layer for the vault; every write operation auto-commits, making git the reversibility substrate.
directional.conclusion: Daftari needs no separate versioning subsystem: every write auto-commits, so history and rollback are already provided.

directional.premise: All files are markdown with YAML frontmatter, and frontmatter is the single metadata layer for the system.
directional.conclusion: Daftari does not need a separate metadata database table for document attributes; the attributes live with the document.

directional.premise: Write locks are file-level and SQLite-backed with a 60-second TTL; an expired lock is released automatically.
directional.conclusion: A daftari write lock left behind by a crashed agent will not block the vault indefinitely.

directional.premise: Only one daftari process may hold a vault at a time; on startup the server SIGTERMs any live holder and waits up to 3 seconds before taking over the per-vault process lock.
directional.conclusion: Two daftari servers cannot end up corrupting the same vault by writing concurrently.

directional.premise: RBAC is config-driven: users and roles are declared in .daftari/config.yaml, not managed at runtime.
directional.conclusion: Daftari ships no user-management UI or user CRUD subsystem.

directional.premise: Edge strength decays on a 90-day half-life from the time of last re-derivation, and the review interval is a function of the aged strength, so a long-unreviewed edge's interval elapses.
directional.conclusion: In Daftari's cortex, a derivation edge that has not been re-examined for a long time should be resurfaced for review.

directional.premise: The monitor validates on a held-out question set, never the set a pass could see, because a pass that could read the exam would optimize to it (the testing-effect Goodhart guard).
directional.conclusion: Daftari's quality monitor evaluates the cortex on questions the curation pass could not have seen.

directional.premise: The envelope lets the human set policy once; the agent auto-acts only inside that policy and surfaces anything outside it for human ratification.
directional.conclusion: Under Daftari's envelope, a high-impact curation action the agent is unsure about is escalated to a human rather than executed.

directional.premise: Krakovna et al. (2018) introduce a path-integrated reachability penalty that charges an agent for cumulative irreversible effects, which the literature map names as the closest prior art to the trust budget.
directional.conclusion: The ATP trust budget has a direct antecedent in the side-effect-penalty literature on agent safety.

directional.premise: The strength gate catches premise-wrong-as-fact while the accumulation budget catches harm from cumulative iteration; the two failures are disjoint, so each gate is blind to the other's failure mode.
directional.conclusion: Daftari needs two separate gates on auto-actions because one gate alone leaves a failure mode open.

directional.premise: Edges are born inside the consolidation loop's birth mode: embedding-neighbor inference merely proposes candidate edges, and the same loop re-validates them by re-derivation, so proposal and validation share one pass.
directional.conclusion: Daftari does not need a separate matcher pipeline to create derivation edges.

directional.premise: The SQLite index is ephemeral and can be rebuilt from the markdown files at any time; the markdown files are the source of truth.
directional.conclusion: Deleting Daftari's .daftari/index.db does not lose any vault data.

directional.premise: ROME's edit formula uses C^-1 as a Mahalanobis preconditioner, treating the input covariance C as the geometry in which factual associations are stored and edited.
directional.conclusion: The thesis analyzes the FFN input covariance C (rather than the raw weight matrices) as the substrate where overshadowing lives.

directional.premise: The default embedding provider runs all-MiniLM-L6-v2 in-process, so embeddings are produced fully locally without any embedding-API call.
directional.conclusion: Daftari can index and search a vault with no external API calls.

directional.premise: Ramani (2026) finds the scaling exponent alpha varies by up to 2.6x with optimizer choice, so alpha encodes the optimization procedure in addition to architecture and data.
directional.conclusion: A model's empirical spectral scaling exponent should be treated as encoding the training procedure, not as an architecture-and-data-determined constant.

directional.premise: Daftari's curation engine is advisory and contradiction-preserving: vault_tension_log records a tension and surfaces it; it deliberately does not resolve, blacklist, or canonicalize-by-majority-vote, because suppressing a live disagreement destroys the signal a human needs.
directional.conclusion: When two curated documents contradict each other, the right action is to keep both live and log the tension rather than pick a winner.

directional.premise: Trust strength moves only on independent re-derivation; usage and popularity are deliberately excluded because reinforcing by successful-use ratio is exactly the Goodhart failure the design forbids.
directional.conclusion: How often a stored claim is used should have no effect on how trustworthy the system considers it.

directional.premise: A literature review found the path-irreversibility theorem already held by Arthur (1989) and Krakovna (2018), so the contribution that survives is the framing and operationalization; claiming a novel theorem would be refuted on the lit map.
directional.conclusion: Recasting the paper as conceptual synthesis rather than a novel theorem strengthens, not weakens, its contribution.

directional.premise: Auto-fixing would violate the never-delete and human-ratify invariants of the trust model: the engine is advisory by design so that a human, not the agent, owns every irreversible change to curated knowledge.
directional.conclusion: Daftari's curation engine should report problems but never automatically fix them.

directional.premise: The vault's invariant is never-delete: wrong or obsolete documents are deprecated or superseded and kept, because git plus provenance make non-destructive revision the reversibility substrate and deletion would erase the audit trail.
directional.conclusion: Daftari never deletes a document, even one judged wrong or obsolete.

directional.premise: Re-reading the prior answer is cramming: it produces a correlated re-derivation that cannot count as independent evidence, so the pass works from premises and the prior tension trail (the generation effect), not the document body verbatim.
directional.conclusion: When re-validating an edge, the pass should re-derive the claim from scratch rather than re-read the document it is checking.

directional.premise: Detecting cumulative overload requires knowing how many write actions have already been taken this session, which is information outside any single forward pass's input; it is an input-availability gap, not a capability gap, so scaling capability does not close it.
directional.conclusion: A more capable model will not, on its own, detect cumulative-action overload within a session.

directional.premise: Under feature superposition, singular vectors of a weight matrix are arbitrary mixtures of features, so singular-value rank does not correspond to feature-popularity rank.
directional.conclusion: Ranking a weight matrix's singular vectors does not give a ranking of knowledge by popularity.

directional.premise: Because git plus never-delete make every action mechanically reversible at near-zero cost, the budget cannot be justified by irreversibility; what it actually rations is the comprehension and coherence load a batch of changes imposes on the human reviewer.
directional.conclusion: Daftari's trust budget exists to bound how much change a human must comprehend, not to bound irreversible damage.

directional.premise: The research aligns specifically with that employer's core agenda (hallucination reduction), so it is a differentiator in that context; broadcasting unfinished, unpublished work universally would weaken rather than strengthen the general application.
directional.conclusion: It is sound to surface one's unpublished research as a credential for one specific employer while omitting it from the resume sent everywhere else.

directional.premise: Immediate re-derivation fights the anti-cramming rule: the inter-session gap is what certifies the next re-derivation independent, so the loop batches at session start rather than reacting live to each write.
directional.conclusion: Daftari deliberately does not fire re-derivation immediately when a document changes.

symmetric.a: The market-clearing price is set at the level where the quantity buyers wish to purchase exactly matches the quantity sellers wish to sell.
symmetric.b: The market-clearing quantity is the volume that changes hands at the single price where the supply and demand schedules intersect.

symmetric.a: The predator population's growth rate rises as prey become more abundant, since more prey means more successful hunts and more offspring.
symmetric.b: The prey population's decline rate rises as predators become more abundant, since more predators means more of the prey are caught.

symmetric.a: Player 1's chosen strategy is a best response given that Player 2 plays the strategy Player 2 has chosen.
symmetric.b: Player 2's chosen strategy is a best response given that Player 1 plays the strategy Player 1 has chosen.

symmetric.a: In special relativity, observer A measures observer B's moving clock to be running slow relative to A's own clock.
symmetric.b: In special relativity, observer B measures observer A's moving clock to be running slow relative to B's own clock.

symmetric.a: The flower's nectar reward and corolla shape evolved to fit the morphology and behavior of its specific pollinator.
symmetric.b: The pollinator's tongue length and foraging behavior evolved to fit the nectar placement and shape of its specific flower.

symmetric.a: The price of one dollar measured in euros equals the reciprocal of the price of one euro measured in dollars.
symmetric.b: The price of one euro measured in dollars equals the reciprocal of the price of one dollar measured in euros.

symmetric.a: Body A orbits the system's barycenter because of the gravitational attraction exerted on it by body B.
symmetric.b: Body B orbits the same barycenter because of the gravitational attraction exerted on it by body A.

symmetric.a: Nation A expands its arsenal each year in direct response to the size of Nation B's arsenal.
symmetric.b: Nation B expands its arsenal each year in direct response to the size of Nation A's arsenal.

symmetric.a: The processor's instruction set was shaped to expose exactly the operations the compiler needed to target.
symmetric.b: The compiler's code generator was shaped to emit exactly the operation mix the processor's instruction set executes fastest.

symmetric.a: In this glossary, the term 'velocity' is defined as an object's rate of change of position with respect to time.
symmetric.b: In this glossary, the phrase 'rate of change of position with respect to time' is precisely what the term 'velocity' labels.
